target.title: fix(ui): reset routed sessions to main on restore
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Control UI could restore a historical routed session key like `agent:*:*:(direct|group):*` from persisted local settings after reload.
- Why it matters: users would reopen Control UI and land in a stale routed session instead of the real main session, making the UI look misrouted even though the backend heartbeat target remained main.
- What changed: session restore now sanitizes persisted routed direct/group session keys during settings load and falls back to `main` instead.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no gateway routing behavior, heartbeat behavior, or live session switching logic was changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #61661
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Control UI restoring historical routed direct/group sessions from persisted local settings instead of falling back to main.
- Real environment tested: local checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` on Linux.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm vitest run ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - `pnpm tsgo:core`
  - `pnpm lint:core`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
```text
✓ ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts ... falls back to main when restoring a routed direct session
✓ ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts ... falls back to main when restoring a routed group session
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       18 passed (18)
```
- Observed result after fix: persisted `agent:*:*:(direct|group):*` session selections are normalized back to `main` during settings restore.
- What was not tested: explicit session selection through URL query params or live backend routing changes, because this PR only changes local restore behavior.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Control UI storage restore trusted persisted `sessionKey` / `lastActiveSessionKey` values without filtering out historical routed direct/group sessions.
- Missing detection / guardrail: storage tests covered scoped session persistence but did not cover stale routed-session restore behavior.
- Contributing context (if known): main-session normalization already existed later in the gateway boot path, but the bad routed session had already won at the storage restore layer.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: persisted routed `direct`/`group` session keys fall back to `main` on Control UI restore.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug originates in local persisted settings load, so the storage tests are the exact boundary where the regression occurs.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): branch adds two restore-specific cases for routed `direct` and `group` sessions.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

After reload/reopen, Control UI will no longer restore stale routed `direct`/`group` sessions from persisted local settings; it will fall back to the main session instead.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Area: Control UI local storage restore

### Steps

1. Seed persisted Control UI settings with a routed direct/group session key in `sessionsByGateway`.
2. Call `loadSettings()`.
3. Verify restored `sessionKey` / `lastActiveSessionKey` fall back to `main`.

### Expected

- routed historical sessions should not win restore by default
- restored settings should point back to main

### Actual

- both new routed-session storage tests pass
- `tsgo:core` passes
- `lint:core` passes

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified the restore sanitization happens in `ui/src/ui/storage.ts`, which is the correct layer for this bug.
- Verified routed `direct` and `group` session keys now restore to `main` in storage tests.
- Verified core type/lint checks pass on the changed files.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a user might intentionally want to reopen a routed direct/group session from persisted state.
  - Mitigation: this PR only changes the automatic restore default; explicit session selection can still be handled through current UI/navigation flows.
- Risk: over-matching could reset legitimate session keys.
  - Mitigation: the sanitization is narrowly limited to `agent:*:*:(direct|group):*` patterns only.



---

## Real behavior proof

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.5 /tmp/pincer-openclaw

 ✓  ui  ../../ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts (22 tests) 38ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  22 passed (22)
    Start at  00:14:43
    Duration  611ms
```

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Routed direct/group session keys persisted in localStorage were not cleared on restore. The regex `/^agent:[^:]+:[^:]+:(direct|group):.+$/` only matched 5-segment per-channel-peer keys, missing `agent:<id>:direct:<peer>` (per-peer) and `agent:<id>:<channel>:<acct>:direct:<peer>` (per-account-channel-peer) shapes emitted by `buildAgentPeerSessionKey`.
**Real environment tested:** Node.js v24.14.0, vitest v4.1.5, jsdom, Linux.
**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts`
**After-fix evidence:** 22/22 tests pass including two new tests covering per-peer (`agent:main:direct:ou_123`) and per-account-channel-peer (`agent:main:feishu:acct_abc:direct:ou_123`) shapes, both correctly falling back to `main`.

target.title: fix(agents/harness): validate forced plugin harness support before pinning
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `selectAgentHarnessDecision()` returned the forced plugin harness for a candidate without consulting that harness's own `supports({ provider, modelId })` predicate. Once an agent's runtime was pinned to a plugin harness (e.g. `agentRuntime.id: "codex"`), every fallback-chain candidate flowed through that harness regardless of whether the harness could actually serve the candidate's provider.
- **Why it matters:** For Codex, this surfaced in production as a late `CodexAppServerRpcError: failed to load configuration: Model provider X not found` whenever an operator's fallback chain referenced an OpenClaw-only provider that was absent from `~/.codex/config.toml`. Observed on operator installs that have a local proxy provider (`9router`) configured under OpenClaw `models.providers` but not registered in Codex's own `[model_providers.*]` config. Combined with the long startup hang fixed in #74335, this turned a single cross-config gap into recurring overnight outages where every agent's tool calls dropped to zero.
- **What changed:** In the forced-plugin branch of `selectAgentHarnessDecision()`, call `forced.supports({ provider, modelId, requestedRuntime })` before pinning. If unsupported, route to PI when fallback policy permits, otherwise throw an unsupported-harness error including the candidate `provider/modelId` and the harness's `reason`. Extend Codex harness `DEFAULT_CODEX_HARNESS_PROVIDER_IDS` from `{"codex"}` to `{"codex", "openai", "openai-codex"}` to match the documented Codex routing ids (`docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md`, `docs/concepts/model-providers.md`, `docs/gateway/config-agents.md`, `docs/plugins/codex-harness.md`) and the existing `resolveCodexAppServerModelProvider()` mappings in `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts`.
- **What did NOT change:** The auto-mode harness selection (`runtime === "auto"`) was already calling `supports()` correctly. Session pinning precedence (`agentHarnessId` over env / config) is preserved. The behavior of `runWithModelFallback()` candidate enumeration is unchanged. Codex app-server protocol is untouched.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #74335 (this repo, the same operator outage's symptom-amplification fix bounding `withCodexStartupTimeout`)
- Related #42715 (Multi-provider AI Gateway: missing apiKey causes silent model fallback) — adjacent class of cross-provider fallback misroute
- Related #48680 (Model fallback treats HTTP 403 business rejection as `candidate_succeeded`) — adjacent class of fallback-decision correctness
- Related #64902 (Failed primary model attempt not logged when fallback succeeds — no forensic audit trail) — orthogonal observability gap
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** `src/agents/harness/selection.ts` forced-plugin branch returned the harness whose `id === runtime` without consulting its `supports()` predicate. The harness's own opinion about which providers it can serve was bypassed entirely whenever the runtime was pinned, so cross-config fallbacks reached Codex's app-server, which then rejected them with `failed to load configuration: Model provider X not found` after sending the request through `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts:97-101`.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** `harness.supports()` exists on every `AgentHarness` and is consulted in the auto-mode branch (`src/agents/harness/selection.ts:134-141`), but the forced branch trusted the runtime id alone. There was no contract test forcing every harness selection path through `supports()`.
- **Contributing context:** Codex's `DEFAULT_CODEX_HARNESS_PROVIDER_IDS` set was historically `{"codex"}` only, even though the documented native path is `openai/gpt-*`. The forced-branch bypass made the narrow set invisible. Tightening the selector (this PR) requires the set to match documented behavior, hence the `openai`/`openai-codex` additions.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- **Coverage level that should have caught this:** Unit test asserting the forced-plugin selection branch consults `supports()` for the candidate `provider/modelId`, plus a contract test for the Codex harness's accepted provider ids.
- **New tests added:**
  - `src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts`: (1) forced plugin harness with unsupported candidate provider falls back to PI when permitted, (2) raises clear error when fallback is `none`, (3) session-pinned plugin harness rejects unsupported candidate provider, (4) compaction routing for a plugin-pinned session whose provider the plugin does not support fails closed.
  - `src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts`: 2 pre-existing pin-precedence tests updated to use a Codex-supported provider so they continue to test their original intent.
  - `extensions/codex/harness.test.ts` (new file): Codex harness `supports()` accepts `codex`, `openai`, `openai-codex` (case-insensitive) and rejects foreign provider ids such as `9router`.

## Architecture / Flow Diagram

```text
Before:
selectAgentHarnessDecision({ provider: "9router", agentRuntime.id: "codex" })
  └─ forced = registered codex harness
  └─ return { harness: codex, ... }              ← supports() never consulted
        └─ Codex app-server: "Model provider 9router not found"   (late RPC error)

After:
selectAgentHarnessDecision({ provider: "9router", agentRuntime.id: "codex" })
  └─ forced = registered codex harness
  └─ forced.supports({ provider: "9router", modelId, requestedRuntime: "codex" })
        ├─ supported: true   → return forced harness          (when codex actually owns it)
        └─ supported: false
              ├─ policy.fallback === "pi"   → return PI harness, log warn
              └─ policy.fallback === "none" → throw "Requested agent harness 'codex' does not support 9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6 (provider is not one of: ...) and PI fallback is disabled."
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 25.3.0 (darwin)
- Runtime/container: npm-global install of `openclaw@2026.4.26`
- Model/provider: `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` primary; `9router` configured as an OpenClaw provider but not in `~/.codex/config.toml`
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A — gateway-internal failure
- Relevant config (redacted): `agents.defaults.model.primary = "openai-codex/gpt-5.5"`; agents pinned to `agentRuntime.id: "codex"`; `models.providers["9router"]` defined under OpenClaw

### Steps

1. Configure an OpenClaw agent with `agentRuntime.id: "codex"` and a fallback chain that synthesizes a `9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6` candidate (via `models.providers["9router"]`).
2. Provoke a primary-model failure (e.g. transient codex startup hang per #73520; or simply force codex offline for a turn).
3. Observe `gateway.err.log`: fallback chain advances `openai-codex/gpt-5.5 → openai/gpt-5.5 → 9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6`; the last candidate fails with `CodexAppServerRpcError: failed to load configuration: Model provider 9router not found` and `next=none`.

### Expected

- The fallback chain skips a candidate the forced harness cannot serve, either routing it to the embedded PI backend (when `policy.fallback === "pi"`) or surfacing a clear unsupported-harness error (when `policy.fallback === "none"`).

### Actual (before this PR)

- Codex receives the unsupported provider and produces the `Model provider X not found` error after the request has already been sent into the harness, with `next=none` ending the chain.

## Evidence

- Failing trace before (extracted from operator's `gateway.err.log`):
  ```text
  2026-04-29T05:40:44.788-05:00 [model-fallback/decision] decision=candidate_failed requested=openai-codex/gpt-5.5 candidate=openai/gpt-5.5 reason=timeout next=9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6
  2026-04-29T05:40:44.792-05:00 [model-fallback/decision] decision=candidate_failed requested=openai-codex/gpt-5.5 candidate=9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6 reason=timeout next=none
  2026-04-29T06:11:15.896-05:00 [diagnostic] lane task error: lane=main durationMs=5082 error="CodexAppServerRpcError: failed to load configuration: Model provider `9router` not found"
  ```
- Passing tests after:
  - `pnpm test src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts` → 26/26 pass.
  - `pnpm test extensions/codex/harness.test.ts` → 4/4 pass.
- Verified locally:
  - `pnpm tsgo:core` clean.
  - `pnpm tsgo:extensions` clean.
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1` clean on all 5 modified files.

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios:**
  - Forced Codex harness with `provider: "openai"` / `modelId: "gpt-5.4"` continues to be pinned (documented canonical path; covered by new codex harness test and unaffected pin-precedence test).
  - Forced Codex harness with `provider: "9router"` and `policy.fallback === "pi"` routes to PI with a warn log; `selectedReason: "forced_plugin_fallback_to_pi"`.
  - Forced Codex harness with `provider: "9router"` and `policy.fallback === "none"` throws `Requested agent harness "codex" does not support 9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6 (provider is not one of: codex, openai, openai-codex) and PI fallback is disabled.`
  - Session-pinned (`agentHarnessId: "codex"`) with unsupported provider throws (covered by new test).
  - Compaction (`maybeCompactAgentHarnessSession`) routes the unsupported pinned-session candidate to the same throw path (covered by new test).
- **Edge cases checked:**
  - Auto-mode selection (`agentRuntime.id: "auto"`) is unchanged; existing tests pass.
  - PI runtime forced (`agentRuntime.id: "pi"`) unchanged.
  - CLI runtime aliases unchanged.
  - `agentHarnessId` (session pin) precedence over `agentRuntime` defaults preserved.
- **What I did NOT verify:**
  - Did not run `pnpm check` (full prod sweep) or `pnpm test` (full suite) locally; relied on targeted test files plus core+extensions typecheck and the changed-files gate. CI should run the broader gates.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` for any deployment where the forced plugin harness already supported the candidate provider via its `supports()` predicate (the documented Codex paths `openai/*`, `openai-codex/*`, `codex/*` continue to pin Codex). Behavior changes only for cross-provider candidates the harness could not have served correctly anyway: those now either route to PI (when permitted) or surface a clear unsupported-harness error instead of producing a late `Model provider X not found` from the underlying harness.
- Config/env changes? `No`.
- Migration needed? `No`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** Some installs may have relied on the forced-pin bypass to route `azure-openai/*` (or another non-canonical id) through Codex's app-server.
  - **Mitigation:** Codex harness can be extended easily — add the additional id to `DEFAULT_CODEX_HARNESS_PROVIDER_IDS` or accept an explicit `providerIds` override on `createCodexAppServerAgentHarness({ providerIds: [...] })`. The current set covers the documented canonical paths; additional ids should be added with explicit owner-extension review.
- **Risk:** The new error message on `policy.fallback === "none"` is surfaced through synchronous selection rather than via a model-fallback decision row.
  - **Mitigation:** The error message is structured (includes provider/modelId/harness id and reason), so log/observation code can match on it. The previous failure mode produced a late `CodexAppServerRpcError` from a different layer, which was harder to attribute to the harness selection decision.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Forced plugin harness selection now consults the harness `supports()` predicate before pinning, so unsupported fallback providers do not get sent into Codex app-server and fail late as "Model provider X not found".
- **Real environment tested:** macOS operator OpenClaw gateway setup, npm-global OpenClaw install, agent pinned to Codex runtime with an OpenClaw-only `9router` fallback provider absent from Codex config. Evidence is from the operator gateway/runtime path described above, with secrets redacted.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the PR branch harness-selection runtime checks and inspected the real gateway failure path/log excerpt that motivated the patch. Commands used: `pnpm test src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts`; `pnpm test extensions/codex/harness.test.ts`; `pnpm tsgo:core`; `pnpm tsgo:extensions`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture / copied live output from the operator setup and PR branch checks:

  ```text
  gateway.err.log before patch (real operator setup):
  2026-04-29T05:40:44.788-05:00 [model-fallback/decision] decision=candidate_failed requested=openai-codex/gpt-5.5 candidate=openai/gpt-5.5 reason=timeout next=9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6
  2026-04-29T05:40:44.792-05:00 [model-fallback/decision] decision=candidate_failed requested=openai-codex/gpt-5.5 candidate=9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6 reason=timeout next=none
  2026-04-29T06:11:15.896-05:00 [diagnostic] lane task error: lane=main durationMs=5082 error="CodexAppServerRpcError: failed to load configuration: Model provider `9router` not found"

  after patch, targeted runtime checks:
  pnpm test src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts -> 26/26 pass
  pnpm test extensions/codex/harness.test.ts -> 4/4 pass
  pnpm tsgo:core -> clean
  pnpm tsgo:extensions -> clean
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The patched branch routes unsupported forced-Codex candidates to PI when fallback is permitted, or throws a clear unsupported-harness error when fallback is disabled, instead of invoking Codex app-server with a provider it cannot load.
- **What was not tested:** Full `pnpm check` / full repository test sweep was not run locally; broader CI should still run before landing.

target.title: feat: per-channel thread.requireExplicitMention override for Slack
target.body:
## Summary

- Add per-channel `thread.requireExplicitMention` override for Slack.
- Preserve current Slack channel controls while rebasing onto current `main`: `allowBots: "mentions"` and `botLoopProtection` remain intact.
- Remove the unrelated dependency-contract relaxation from this PR scope.

## Use Case

An operator wants some Slack channels, such as client-facing channels, to require `@bot` on every thread reply while internal channels keep the default "mention once, then auto-participate" behavior.

## Changes

**`src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts`**
- Add channel-level `thread.requireExplicitMention` via `SlackChannelThreadSchema`.
- Keep `allowBots: boolean | "mentions"` and `botLoopProtection` on `SlackChannelSchema`.

**`extensions/slack/src/monitor/channel-config.ts`**
- Add `threadRequireExplicitMention` to resolved channel config.
- Resolve with fallback order: channel entry -> wildcard entry -> account default.

**`extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.ts`**
- Pass the account default into channel config resolution.
- Use the effective channel policy before collecting implicit thread mentions.
- Use the same effective channel policy for activation gating.

**`extensions/slack/src/monitor/auth.ts` / `slash.ts` / `context.ts`**
- Thread policy resolution uses the same account-default fallback path.

**Tests / generated docs**
- Add coverage for channel override true and false.
- Regenerate bundled channel config metadata and config docs baseline.

## Config Example

```json
{
  "channels": {
    "slack": {
      "accounts": {
        "mybot": {
          "thread": { "requireExplicitMention": false },
          "channels": {
            "C0CLIENT01": {
              "enabled": true,
              "requireMention": true,
              "thread": { "requireExplicitMention": true }
            },
            "C0INTERNAL01": {
              "enabled": true,
              "requireMention": true,
              "thread": { "requireExplicitMention": false }
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke`
- [x] `pnpm config:channels:check`
- [x] `pnpm config:docs:check`
- [x] `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/monitor.test.ts` - 2 files / 109 tests passed
- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt --check CHANGELOG.md docs/channels/slack.md extensions/slack/src/monitor/auth.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/channel-config.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/context.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/monitor.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/slash.ts src/config/types.slack.ts src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts`
- [ ] Maintainer-run Mantis Slack desktop smoke or equivalent live Slack proof, if maintainers require browser-visible transport evidence before merge.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Per-channel Slack `thread.requireExplicitMention` now overrides the account-level default for both early implicit-thread mention collection and later activation gating, while preserving current `allowBots: "mentions"` and `botLoopProtection` behavior.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout at `/Users/shannon/repos/openclaw` on macOS Darwin arm64, Node v22.22.2, pnpm v11.1.0; branch `feat/slack-channel-thread-mention-override-rebased`, exact head `3f9899d43630f3d41a133b21310d5cf2c00572e5`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Replayed the Slack monitor preparation path with the focused Slack monitor suites and regenerated/checked config surfaces:
  ```text
  pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke
  pnpm config:channels:check
  pnpm config:docs:check
  pnpm exec vitest run extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/monitor.test.ts

  $ pnpm build:plugin-sdk:dts && node --experimental-strip-types scripts/write-plugin-sdk-entry-dts.ts

target.title: fix(errors): dedupe identical messages when traversing error .cause chain
target.body: See before and after in these 2 messages: ## Summary - `formatErrorMessage` walked `.cause` and appended every level joined by ` | `, so a wrapper that carried the same string as its cause produced doubled output. - `coerceToFailoverError` (`src/agents/failover-error.ts:524-563`) does exactly that: it builds a `FailoverError` whose `message` is read from the wrapped error and sets `cause: err`. That made auth failures like `No API key found for provider "openai-codex" …` surface in the TUI as the same sentence twice, separated by ` | `, inside the ⚠️ Agent failed before reply line. - Now the formatter tracks already-emitted messages and skips repeats — leaving genuinely-different cause messages intact (still joined with ` | `). Behavior addressed: TUI control-UI failure path renders the error message once instead of duplicated. Underlying auth misconfiguration is unchanged. ## Test plan - [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/errors.test.ts` (added a regression case covering the FailoverError-style wrap).

target.title: fix(auto-reply): enforce word boundary in slash command prefix match
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #84572.

`parseSlashCommandActionArgs` used a naive `startsWith` against the configured slash prefix. When a skill name shares a prefix with a built-in command, the longer name was captured by the shorter built-in handler.

Reporter's example: a skill named `config-check` invoked via `/config-check <args>` was captured by the built-in `/config` handler and surfaced as:

> ⚠️  /config is disabled. Set commands.config=true to enable.

Any skill whose name starts with a built-in command prefix (`config-*`, `debug-*`, `models-*`, etc.) was unreachable via slash invocation from any channel.

## Fix

After the prefix match, require that the character immediately after the matched prefix is whitespace, a colon, or end-of-string. Otherwise the prefix collided with a longer command name → return `no-match` so the longer handler — or the skill router — gets a chance to claim it.

```diff
   if (!normalizeLowercaseStringOrEmpty(trimmed).startsWith(slashLower)) {
     return { kind: "no-match" };
   }
+  const charAfter = trimmed.charAt(slash.length);
+  if (charAfter && !/[\s:]/.test(charAfter)) {
+    return { kind: "no-match" };
+  }
   const rest = trimmed.slice(slash.length).trim();
```

This is exactly the suggested fix from the issue body.

## Tests

Adds `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-slash-parse.test.ts` covering:

- `/config-check <args>` returns null for `/config` (the reported case)
- `/configfoo` (no separator) returns null
- `/modelsy` returns null for `/models` (no boundary)
- `/config:json` still matches (colon is a valid boundary)
- `/config show enabled` still parses cleanly (whitespace boundary)
- empty body still returns the default action
- exact-prefix with leading whitespace still parses

## Impact

Closes a regression that affected any skill whose name shares a prefix with a built-in command. Restores reachability for `/config-check`, `/debug-trace`, `/models-list`, etc.

target.title: fix(minimax): pass duration field to music_generation API
target.body:
## Summary
- Add the `duration` JSON field to the MiniMax `/v1/music_generation` request body when `durationSeconds` is provided.
- Previously, `durationSeconds` was only embedded as a natural-language hint in the prompt text, which the MiniMax API ignores — producing default-length tracks instead of the requested duration.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: MiniMax music_generation provider does not pass `durationSeconds` to the API. It appends a prompt hint like "Target duration: about 30 seconds." which the API ignores, silently producing default-length tracks.

**Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node.js v22.22.0. Pure JavaScript object construction, platform-independent.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch**:
```bash
cd openclaw-84473
node proof_repro_84508.mjs
```
The script builds the API request body with the old and new logic, testing 4 scenarios: with durationSeconds, without, NaN edge case, and float rounding.

**Evidence after fix**:
```
=== MiniMax music_generation durationSeconds fix (#84508) ===
Timestamp : 2026-05-20T12:52:48.657Z
Node.js   : v22.22.0
Platform  : linux x64

--- Scenario 1: request with durationSeconds=30 ---
BEFORE fix: "duration" in body = false
AFTER  fix: "duration" in body = true, value = 30
PASS: true

--- Scenario 2: request without durationSeconds ---
AFTER  fix: "duration" in body = false
PASS: true

--- Scenario 3: durationSeconds=NaN (edge case) ---
AFTER  fix: "duration" in body = false
PASS: true

--- Scenario 4: durationSeconds=45.7 (rounded) ---
AFTER  fix: duration = 46
PASS: true

=== Result ===
With durationSeconds  : PASS
Without durationSeconds: PASS
NaN edge case          : PASS
Float rounding         : PASS
Overall                : PASS
```

**Observed result after fix**: Before fix: the API request body has no `duration` field, so MiniMax returns default-length tracks. After fix: `duration: 30` is included in the body when `durationSeconds=30` is requested. Requests without `durationSeconds` or with NaN are unaffected.

**What was not tested**: Live MiniMax API call with real credentials. The prompt-embedded duration hint is intentionally left in place as a fallback hint.

Fixes #84508

target.title: feat(telegram): interleave CLI tool-progress + reasoning + rolling timer in one Telegram message
target.body:
> **Rescope (2026-05-20):** This PR previously also added a parallel CLI tool-event emitter in `cli-output.ts` and a followup-path bridge in `agent-runner-execution.ts`. Those overlap with [#80046](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/80046) which adds the canonical CLI tool-event surface (`dispatchClaudeCliStreamingToolEvent` + `onToolUseStart` / `onToolResult` parser callbacks, three claude `stream-json` record shapes, args+result sanitisation, isCliProvider-gated bridge). Landing both would have shipped duplicate emitters with divergent contracts. Commit `70496edf7c` reverts those overlapping changes; #82285 now reduces to the **Telegram-side rendering layer** that consumes the agent-event bus #80046 publishes. Depends on #80046 landing first.

---

## Summary

- **Problem.** When Claude calls a tool mid-turn on the `claude-cli` backend, the assistant text stream just pauses — often for tens of seconds on `Read` / `Bash` / `Grep` / `WebFetch`. The end user sees a frozen typing indicator and no signal about which tool is running.
- **Why it matters.** Looks like the gateway hung; users abandon turns that are still in flight. Tool calls live in `stream_event` records, never in `assistant_text` deltas, so the existing assistant-stream consumer never surfaces them.
- **What changed.** Two complementary surfaces on `createCliJsonlStreamingParser` (gated by `usesClaudeStreamJsonDialect`):
  1. **In-stream textual marker** via `onAssistantDelta` — `\n\n[HH:MM:SS] 🛠️ ToolName: detail\n` followed by a rolling `_ Ns — HH:MM:SS_` indicator that refreshes every 8s, stripped on the next `text_delta` or on turn end.
  2. **Structured `stream: "tool"` agent event** (`{phase: "start", name, args, itemId}`) via a new optional `onToolEvent` callback — consumed by `cli-runner/execute.ts` and forwarded to the agent event bus for downstream tool-tile UIs.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** Gated by `usesClaudeStreamJsonDialect({backend, providerId})` — no impact on non-Claude CLI backends. Existing `onAssistantDelta` callers are unaffected. `onToolEvent` is opt-in. No tool-output / tool-result event in this PR (the result is reflected in claude's next `text_delta` and the timer-strip handles UX). Tool args sanitized via `sanitizeToolArgs` before either path.
- **AI-assisted:** yes — drafted with Claude.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** tool calls during a Claude turn now produce both an in-stream textual marker (with rolling 8s timer indicator) and a structured `stream: "tool"` agent event, when `backend.jsonlDialect === "claude-stream-json"` or `providerId === "claude-cli"`.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` v2.1.142, Opus 4.7, live OpenClaw gateway driving the `claude-cli` backend per-turn over Telegram.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json`; live Telegram turn that invokes one or more tools (Read / Bash / Grep / WebFetch); observe the textual marker, the 8s tick refresh, and the marker stripping plus `\n\n` separator when assistant text resumes; verify the `stream: "tool"` agent event in `emitAgentEvent` logs.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - Telegram screenshot of a live turn (rolling-timer rendered to the end-user surface): ![telegram rolling-timer](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-assets/pr-82285/.proof/telegram-rolling-timer.jpg). Every `[HH:MM:SS] 🛠️ Bash: …` line in the screenshot is one `emitTool` firing in `createClaudeToolUseTracker` against `claude-cli`.
  - Typecheck clean: `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json`.
  - The markers are an in-flight overlay delivered via `onAssistantDelta`, not persisted to `~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/*.jsonl` (which stores claude's structured `result`) or `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log` at default verbosity — Telegram is the canonical capture surface.
- **Observed result after fix:** tool calls render as `[HH:MM:SS] 🛠️ ToolName: detail` blocks in the assistant text, with the live timer ticking every 8s. When claude resumes streaming text, the timer is stripped and the text continues from a clean `\n\n` boundary. Agent-event consumers receive a structured `{phase: "start", name, args, itemId}` for each tool call.
- **What was not tested:** end-to-end against a dashboard / external listener for the `stream: "tool"` event surface (event is emitted, visible in `emitAgentEvent` logs, but no downstream UI verified beyond that); Linux/macOS execution (developed on Windows only); bundled MCP tool paths (`server_tool_use` / `mcp_tool_use` content-block types are matched in the tracker but I have not driven a live MCP-tool turn through it); focused unit test for `createClaudeToolUseTracker` is not added yet (see Regression Test Plan).

## Change Type

- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked

- Adds opt-in `onToolEvent` callback on `createCliJsonlStreamingParser` + in-stream textual marker over the existing `onAssistantDelta`.
- Overlap to coordinate: #80046 (CLI tool-event bridge). Maintainers' judgment on whether inline rolling markers ride alongside or after the structured bridge.

## Root Cause

N/A — feature PR. The condition this addresses (no tool-call UX during turns) is a property of the Claude CLI JSONL surface (tool calls live in `stream_event`, never in `assistant_text` deltas), not a regression in OpenClaw.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target:** `src/agents/cli-output.test.ts` — add a `describe("tool-use tracker", …)` block.
- **Scenario locked in:** simulated `stream_event` sequence — `message_start` → `content_block_start (tool_use, name: "Read")` → 3× `content_block_delta (input_json_delta partial)` → `content_block_stop` — asserts the resulting `ClaudeToolEvent` carries the reassembled `args.file_path`, the right `itemId`, and that the parser's `assistantText` now includes the `[HH:MM:SS] 🛠️ Read: <path>` line.
- **Why smallest reliable guardrail:** the failure mode (regression of partial-JSON accumulation, wrong `content_block_stop` dispatch, verbose-gating drift) is fully exercisable by calling the parser's `push()` with a canned JSONL transcript — no live claude needed. Runs in ms.
- **Honest gap:** I have **not** added this test yet. Behaviour is exercised live in my local environment but a frozen-transcript unit test belongs with this PR before flipping out of draft. Flagging as a must-add before un-drafting.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** partial-JSON accumulation in `createClaudeToolUseTracker` could drop or corrupt tool args if `content_block_stop` fires before all `input_json_delta` chunks arrive.
  - **Mitigation:** tracker holds the entry until `content_block_stop`, then parses `entry.input ?? entry.partialJson` via `parseClaudeToolArgs`. The pending unit test (`cli-output.test.ts`) locks in the contract for partial-JSON reassembly.
- **Risk:** rolling-timer `setInterval` leaks if a `content_block_stop` arrives but no subsequent `text_delta` fires (e.g. turn aborts mid-tool).
  - **Mitigation:** `clearToolKeepalive()` is also called on `result`, on `finish()`, and on the next `text_delta`. The parser closure owns the interval handle and explicitly clears it on every terminal path.
- **Risk:** tool args contain sensitive content (file paths, credentials in `WebFetch` URLs, etc.) and surface in both Telegram and agent events.
  - **Mitigation:** `sanitizeToolArgs` truncates and redacts known-sensitive keys before either emission path. Verbose gating (`shouldInjectToolInlineMarkers`) lets sessions opt out of inline markers without losing the structured event.
- **Risk:** the inline marker rides on `onAssistantDelta` — any consumer that strict-parses assistant text as Markdown could choke on the `_ Ns — HH:MM:SS_` indicator.
  - **Mitigation:** marker uses a deliberately conservative `\n\n[…] 🛠️ …\n` shape that's Markdown-safe (Telegram renders it verbatim). Consumers wanting strict text can ignore by subscribing only to the structured `stream: "tool"` event.

## Security Impact

- **New permissions/capabilities:** No.
- **Secrets/tokens handling changed:** No — same allowed-tools set; `sanitizeToolArgs` truncates args before emission.
- **New/changed network calls:** No.
- **Command/tool execution surface changed:** No — same tools claude already executes; this PR only renders that they're in flight.
- **Data access scope changed:** No — tracker reads `stream_event` records the parser already processes.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible: gated by `usesClaudeStreamJsonDialect`; non-Claude backends unaffected.
- `onToolEvent` is an additive optional callback — existing parser callers don't need to wire it up.
- No new config, no new env vars. Disable by not subscribing to the new event surface.

commands.author: cathrynlavery

target.title: fix(agents/harness): validate forced plugin harness support before pinning
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `selectAgentHarnessDecision()` returned the forced plugin harness for a candidate without consulting that harness's own `supports({ provider, modelId })` predicate. Once an agent's runtime was pinned to a plugin harness (e.g. `agentRuntime.id: "codex"`), every fallback-chain candidate flowed through that harness regardless of whether the harness could actually serve the candidate's provider.
- **Why it matters:** For Codex, this surfaced in production as a late `CodexAppServerRpcError: failed to load configuration: Model provider X not found` whenever an operator's fallback chain referenced an OpenClaw-only provider that was absent from `~/.codex/config.toml`. Observed on operator installs that have a local proxy provider (`9router`) configured under OpenClaw `models.providers` but not registered in Codex's own `[model_providers.*]` config. Combined with the long startup hang fixed in #74335, this turned a single cross-config gap into recurring overnight outages where every agent's tool calls dropped to zero.
- **What changed:** In the forced-plugin branch of `selectAgentHarnessDecision()`, call `forced.supports({ provider, modelId, requestedRuntime })` before pinning. If unsupported, route to PI when fallback policy permits, otherwise throw an unsupported-harness error including the candidate `provider/modelId` and the harness's `reason`. Extend Codex harness `DEFAULT_CODEX_HARNESS_PROVIDER_IDS` from `{"codex"}` to `{"codex", "openai", "openai-codex"}` to match the documented Codex routing ids (`docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md`, `docs/concepts/model-providers.md`, `docs/gateway/config-agents.md`, `docs/plugins/codex-harness.md`) and the existing `resolveCodexAppServerModelProvider()` mappings in `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts`.
- **What did NOT change:** The auto-mode harness selection (`runtime === "auto"`) was already calling `supports()` correctly. Session pinning precedence (`agentHarnessId` over env / config) is preserved. The behavior of `runWithModelFallback()` candidate enumeration is unchanged. Codex app-server protocol is untouched.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #74335 (this repo, the same operator outage's symptom-amplification fix bounding `withCodexStartupTimeout`)
- Related #42715 (Multi-provider AI Gateway: missing apiKey causes silent model fallback) — adjacent class of cross-provider fallback misroute
- Related #48680 (Model fallback treats HTTP 403 business rejection as `candidate_succeeded`) — adjacent class of fallback-decision correctness
- Related #64902 (Failed primary model attempt not logged when fallback succeeds — no forensic audit trail) — orthogonal observability gap
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** `src/agents/harness/selection.ts` forced-plugin branch returned the harness whose `id === runtime` without consulting its `supports()` predicate. The harness's own opinion about which providers it can serve was bypassed entirely whenever the runtime was pinned, so cross-config fallbacks reached Codex's app-server, which then rejected them with `failed to load configuration: Model provider X not found` after sending the request through `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts:97-101`.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** `harness.supports()` exists on every `AgentHarness` and is consulted in the auto-mode branch (`src/agents/harness/selection.ts:134-141`), but the forced branch trusted the runtime id alone. There was no contract test forcing every harness selection path through `supports()`.
- **Contributing context:** Codex's `DEFAULT_CODEX_HARNESS_PROVIDER_IDS` set was historically `{"codex"}` only, even though the documented native path is `openai/gpt-*`. The forced-branch bypass made the narrow set invisible. Tightening the selector (this PR) requires the set to match documented behavior, hence the `openai`/`openai-codex` additions.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- **Coverage level that should have caught this:** Unit test asserting the forced-plugin selection branch consults `supports()` for the candidate `provider/modelId`, plus a contract test for the Codex harness's accepted provider ids.
- **New tests added:**
  - `src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts`: (1) forced plugin harness with unsupported candidate provider falls back to PI when permitted, (2) raises clear error when fallback is `none`, (3) session-pinned plugin harness rejects unsupported candidate provider, (4) compaction routing for a plugin-pinned session whose provider the plugin does not support fails closed.
  - `src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts`: 2 pre-existing pin-precedence tests updated to use a Codex-supported provider so they continue to test their original intent.
  - `extensions/codex/harness.test.ts` (new file): Codex harness `supports()` accepts `codex`, `openai`, `openai-codex` (case-insensitive) and rejects foreign provider ids such as `9router`.

## Architecture / Flow Diagram

```text
Before:
selectAgentHarnessDecision({ provider: "9router", agentRuntime.id: "codex" })
  └─ forced = registered codex harness
  └─ return { harness: codex, ... }              ← supports() never consulted
        └─ Codex app-server: "Model provider 9router not found"   (late RPC error)

After:
selectAgentHarnessDecision({ provider: "9router", agentRuntime.id: "codex" })
  └─ forced = registered codex harness
  └─ forced.supports({ provider: "9router", modelId, requestedRuntime: "codex" })
        ├─ supported: true   → return forced harness          (when codex actually owns it)
        └─ supported: false
              ├─ policy.fallback === "pi"   → return PI harness, log warn
              └─ policy.fallback === "none" → throw "Requested agent harness 'codex' does not support 9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6 (provider is not one of: ...) and PI fallback is disabled."
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 25.3.0 (darwin)
- Runtime/container: npm-global install of `openclaw@2026.4.26`
- Model/provider: `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` primary; `9router` configured as an OpenClaw provider but not in `~/.codex/config.toml`
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A — gateway-internal failure
- Relevant config (redacted): `agents.defaults.model.primary = "openai-codex/gpt-5.5"`; agents pinned to `agentRuntime.id: "codex"`; `models.providers["9router"]` defined under OpenClaw

### Steps

1. Configure an OpenClaw agent with `agentRuntime.id: "codex"` and a fallback chain that synthesizes a `9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6` candidate (via `models.providers["9router"]`).
2. Provoke a primary-model failure (e.g. transient codex startup hang per #73520; or simply force codex offline for a turn).
3. Observe `gateway.err.log`: fallback chain advances `openai-codex/gpt-5.5 → openai/gpt-5.5 → 9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6`; the last candidate fails with `CodexAppServerRpcError: failed to load configuration: Model provider 9router not found` and `next=none`.

### Expected

- The fallback chain skips a candidate the forced harness cannot serve, either routing it to the embedded PI backend (when `policy.fallback === "pi"`) or surfacing a clear unsupported-harness error (when `policy.fallback === "none"`).

### Actual (before this PR)

- Codex receives the unsupported provider and produces the `Model provider X not found` error after the request has already been sent into the harness, with `next=none` ending the chain.

## Evidence

- Failing trace before (extracted from operator's `gateway.err.log`):
  ```text
  2026-04-29T05:40:44.788-05:00 [model-fallback/decision] decision=candidate_failed requested=openai-codex/gpt-5.5 candidate=openai/gpt-5.5 reason=timeout next=9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6
  2026-04-29T05:40:44.792-05:00 [model-fallback/decision] decision=candidate_failed requested=openai-codex/gpt-5.5 candidate=9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6 reason=timeout next=none
  2026-04-29T06:11:15.896-05:00 [diagnostic] lane task error: lane=main durationMs=5082 error="CodexAppServerRpcError: failed to load configuration: Model provider `9router` not found"
  ```
- Passing tests after:
  - `pnpm test src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts` → 26/26 pass.
  - `pnpm test extensions/codex/harness.test.ts` → 4/4 pass.
- Verified locally:
  - `pnpm tsgo:core` clean.
  - `pnpm tsgo:extensions` clean.
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1` clean on all 5 modified files.

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios:**
  - Forced Codex harness with `provider: "openai"` / `modelId: "gpt-5.4"` continues to be pinned (documented canonical path; covered by new codex harness test and unaffected pin-precedence test).
  - Forced Codex harness with `provider: "9router"` and `policy.fallback === "pi"` routes to PI with a warn log; `selectedReason: "forced_plugin_fallback_to_pi"`.
  - Forced Codex harness with `provider: "9router"` and `policy.fallback === "none"` throws `Requested agent harness "codex" does not support 9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6 (provider is not one of: codex, openai, openai-codex) and PI fallback is disabled.`
  - Session-pinned (`agentHarnessId: "codex"`) with unsupported provider throws (covered by new test).
  - Compaction (`maybeCompactAgentHarnessSession`) routes the unsupported pinned-session candidate to the same throw path (covered by new test).
- **Edge cases checked:**
  - Auto-mode selection (`agentRuntime.id: "auto"`) is unchanged; existing tests pass.
  - PI runtime forced (`agentRuntime.id: "pi"`) unchanged.
  - CLI runtime aliases unchanged.
  - `agentHarnessId` (session pin) precedence over `agentRuntime` defaults preserved.
- **What I did NOT verify:**
  - Did not run `pnpm check` (full prod sweep) or `pnpm test` (full suite) locally; relied on targeted test files plus core+extensions typecheck and the changed-files gate. CI should run the broader gates.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` for any deployment where the forced plugin harness already supported the candidate provider via its `supports()` predicate (the documented Codex paths `openai/*`, `openai-codex/*`, `codex/*` continue to pin Codex). Behavior changes only for cross-provider candidates the harness could not have served correctly anyway: those now either route to PI (when permitted) or surface a clear unsupported-harness error instead of producing a late `Model provider X not found` from the underlying harness.
- Config/env changes? `No`.
- Migration needed? `No`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** Some installs may have relied on the forced-pin bypass to route `azure-openai/*` (or another non-canonical id) through Codex's app-server.
  - **Mitigation:** Codex harness can be extended easily — add the additional id to `DEFAULT_CODEX_HARNESS_PROVIDER_IDS` or accept an explicit `providerIds` override on `createCodexAppServerAgentHarness({ providerIds: [...] })`. The current set covers the documented canonical paths; additional ids should be added with explicit owner-extension review.
- **Risk:** The new error message on `policy.fallback === "none"` is surfaced through synchronous selection rather than via a model-fallback decision row.
  - **Mitigation:** The error message is structured (includes provider/modelId/harness id and reason), so log/observation code can match on it. The previous failure mode produced a late `CodexAppServerRpcError` from a different layer, which was harder to attribute to the harness selection decision.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Forced plugin harness selection now consults the harness `supports()` predicate before pinning, so unsupported fallback providers do not get sent into Codex app-server and fail late as "Model provider X not found".
- **Real environment tested:** macOS operator OpenClaw gateway setup, npm-global OpenClaw install, agent pinned to Codex runtime with an OpenClaw-only `9router` fallback provider absent from Codex config. Evidence is from the operator gateway/runtime path described above, with secrets redacted.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the PR branch harness-selection runtime checks and inspected the real gateway failure path/log excerpt that motivated the patch. Commands used: `pnpm test src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts`; `pnpm test extensions/codex/harness.test.ts`; `pnpm tsgo:core`; `pnpm tsgo:extensions`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture / copied live output from the operator setup and PR branch checks:

  ```text
  gateway.err.log before patch (real operator setup):
  2026-04-29T05:40:44.788-05:00 [model-fallback/decision] decision=candidate_failed requested=openai-codex/gpt-5.5 candidate=openai/gpt-5.5 reason=timeout next=9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6
  2026-04-29T05:40:44.792-05:00 [model-fallback/decision] decision=candidate_failed requested=openai-codex/gpt-5.5 candidate=9router/cc/claude-opus-4-6 reason=timeout next=none
  2026-04-29T06:11:15.896-05:00 [diagnostic] lane task error: lane=main durationMs=5082 error="CodexAppServerRpcError: failed to load configuration: Model provider `9router` not found"

  after patch, targeted runtime checks:
  pnpm test src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts -> 26/26 pass
  pnpm test extensions/codex/harness.test.ts -> 4/4 pass
  pnpm tsgo:core -> clean
  pnpm tsgo:extensions -> clean
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The patched branch routes unsupported forced-Codex candidates to PI when fallback is permitted, or throws a clear unsupported-harness error when fallback is disabled, instead of invoking Codex app-server with a provider it cannot load.
- **What was not tested:** Full `pnpm check` / full repository test sweep was not run locally; broader CI should still run before landing.

target.title: fix(telegram): recognize bot_command entities as explicit mentions in mention gate
target.body:
## Summary
- Extend `hasBotMention()` to check `bot_command` entities (e.g. `/ping@TargetBot`) in addition to `mention` entities.
- Previously, Telegram group messages with `/command@TargetBot` were dropped by the mention gate as `no-mention` when `requireMention: true`, because the Bot API represents these as `bot_command` entities, not `mention` entities.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram bot-to-bot `/command@TargetBot` messages are dropped by the mention gate in groups with `requireMention: true`. The Bot API represents these as `bot_command` entities, but the mention gate only checks `mention` entities.

**Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node.js v22.22.0. Pure JavaScript string/entity parsing logic, platform-independent.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch**:
```bash
cd openclaw-84473
node proof_repro_84462.mjs
```
The script tests the mention detection function with 5 scenarios: bot_command targeting our bot, case-insensitive match, normal mention entity, bot_command targeting another bot, and plain text without mention.

**Evidence after fix**:
```
=== Telegram bot_command mention gate fix (#84462) ===
Timestamp : 2026-05-20T13:02:33.846Z
Node.js   : v22.22.0
Platform  : linux x64

--- /ping@mybot (bot_command entity) ---
  BEFORE: no-mention (expect: mentioned)
  AFTER : mentioned → PASS

--- /help@MyBot (case-insensitive) ---
  BEFORE: no-mention (expect: mentioned)
  AFTER : mentioned → PASS

--- @mybot (mention entity) ---
  BEFORE: mentioned (expect: mentioned)
  AFTER : mentioned → PASS

--- /ping@otherbot (not our bot) ---
  BEFORE: no-mention (expect: no-mention)
  AFTER : no-mention → PASS

--- plain text without mention ---
  BEFORE: no-mention (expect: no-mention)
  AFTER : no-mention → PASS

=== Result ===
All scenarios: PASS
```

**Observed result after fix**: Before fix: `/ping@mybot` with a `bot_command` entity returns `no-mention`, causing the message to be silently dropped. After fix: the `bot_command` entity text is checked for `@botUsername` suffix (case-insensitive), so `/ping@mybot` is correctly recognized as an explicit mention. Normal mentions and non-targeting commands are unaffected.

**What was not tested**: Live Telegram Bot API integration with real bot credentials. Forum/topic-specific routing. The second call site in `bot-handlers.runtime.ts:690` (which uses the same `hasBotMention` function, so the fix applies there too).

Fixes #84462

target.title: Codex/issue 63523 slack download auth
target.body:
## Summary

* Problem: Slack `download-file` 在文件 URL 跳转到 Slack CDN 时，只在首跳带了 `Authorization`，后续跳转请求丢掉了 token，最终拿到 HTML 登录页而不是文件内容。
* Why it matters: 这会让已经具备 `files:read` 权限的 Slack bot 仍然无法下载文件，用户只能看到 “File could not be downloaded”。
* What changed: 调整 Slack 媒体下载请求逻辑，只要重定向目标仍然是 Slack 自有 HTTPS 域名，就继续附带 `Authorization`；如果跳出 Slack 域，则移除该 header。并补充回归测试覆盖该行为。
* What did NOT change (scope boundary): 没有修改 Slack 权限模型、没有扩大 token 可发送的域范围、没有改动非 Slack 渠道或通用下载框架。

## Change Type (select all)

* [x]  Bug fix
* [ ]  Feature
* [ ]  Refactor required for the fix
* [ ]  Docs
* [ ]  Security hardening
* [ ]  Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

* [ ]  Gateway / orchestration
* [ ]  Skills / tool execution
* [x]  Auth / tokens
* [ ]  Memory / storage
* [x]  Integrations
* [ ]  API / contracts
* [ ]  UI / DX
* [ ]  CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

* Closes #63523
* Related #
* [x]  This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

* Root cause: Slack 下载路径里的自定义 fetch 只在第一次请求时附带 `Authorization`。`fetchRemoteMedia` 手动跟随 302 时，第二跳如果仍落在 Slack CDN，就会无 token 请求，返回 HTML 登录页。
* Missing detection / guardrail: 缺少覆盖 “Slack -> Slack CDN 重定向仍需带 auth” 的回归测试；已有逻辑默认把跨源跳转都当成“不需要 token”。
* Contributing context (if known): Node/fetch 在跨源重定向时默认不会保留 `Authorization`，这里原本是手动绕过这个行为，但把 Slack CDN 场景建模得过于宽松了。

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

* Coverage level that should have caught this:
  * [x]  Unit test
  * [ ]  Seam / integration test
  * [ ]  End-to-end test
  * [ ]  Existing coverage already sufficient
* Target test or file: `extensions/slack/src/monitor/media.test.ts`
* Scenario the test should lock in: Slack 文件下载在 `files.slack.com` 返回 302 到 `cdn.slack-edge.com` 时，第二跳仍然带 `Authorization`；如果跳转离开 Slack 域，则移除该 header。
* Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: 这个问题完全发生在请求构造和重定向处理层，用 mock fetch 的单测就能稳定复现，不需要真实 Slack 环境。
* Existing test that already covers this (if any): 无；本 PR 新增/更新了对应覆盖。
* If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Slack `download-file` 在 Slack 自有 CDN 重定向场景下会恢复正常下载，不再错误返回 “File could not be downloaded (not found, too large, or inaccessible)”。无新增配置项，无默认值变化。

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[download-file] -> [files.slack.com 302] -> [cdn.slack-edge.com without Authorization] -> [HTML login page] -> [download fails]

After:
[download-file] -> [files.slack.com 302] -> [Slack-owned redirect keeps Authorization] -> [binary file response] -> [download succeeds]

target.title: [AI-assisted] fix(plugins): scope startup metadata manifest reads
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Gateway startup could still rebuild plugin metadata from every installed plugin manifest before narrowing to the configured startup set.
- Solution: carry a conservative startup plugin-id scope into metadata snapshot loading when the Installed Plugin Index can prove the narrowed set safely.
- What changed: metadata snapshots can carry scoped `pluginIds`; lookup/startup paths pass a conservative `pluginIdScope`; scoped snapshots are not reused for configless default discovery.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no plugin config shape changes, no provider/channel activation semantics changes, no compat bundled-discovery shortcut.

## Motivation

#70533 is a startup stability problem: a restrictive plugin setup should not crash-loop or pay full manifest reconstruction cost just because unrelated installed plugins exist. This keeps the startup path on the Installed Plugin Index when it can safely prove the manifest subset, and falls back to the old unscoped behavior when it cannot.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #70533
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: restrictive gateway startup plugin scope still caused full installed-index manifest reconstruction before startup narrowing.
- Real environment tested: isolated Linux testserver disposable repo, Node v22.22.2, pnpm 11.1.2, `origin/main` at `a13468320c` compared with this patch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran a TSX diagnostic script through the real `loadPluginLookUpTable -> loadPluginMetadataSnapshot -> loadPluginManifestRegistryForInstalledIndex -> resolveGatewayStartupPluginPlanFromRegistry` path using a valid 96-plugin Installed Plugin Index fixture and `plugins.allow=["openai"]`.
- Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "label": "after-rebased-patch-confirm",
  "indexPluginCount": 96,
  "manifestPluginCount": 1,
  "startupPluginIds": ["openai"],
  "loadedPluginIds": ["openai"],
  "diagnosticCount": 0
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the Installed Plugin Index still contains 96 plugins, but metadata manifest reconstruction reads only the one allowed startup plugin manifest; startup selection remains `["openai"]`.
- What was not tested: no production credentials, no persistent services, no real external provider/channel account.
- Before evidence:

```json
{
  "label": "before-rebased-origin-main",
  "indexPluginCount": 96,
  "manifestPluginCount": 96,
  "startupPluginIds": ["openai"],
  "diagnosticCount": 0
}
```

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: metadata snapshot loading had no way to receive a conservative startup plugin-id scope after the Installed Plugin Index was available, so it reconstructed the installed manifest registry for the full index before startup planning narrowed plugin ids.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered startup narrowing and metadata memoization separately, but not the manifest reconstruction count for restrictive gateway startup allowlists.
- Contributing context (if known): some startup intent, channel, provider, slot, and harness decisions can only be trusted when the Installed Plugin Index can map them without reading manifests; unsafe cases need to stay unscoped.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/commands/status.summary.redaction.test.ts`
  - `src/plugins/plugin-lookup-table.test.ts`
  - `src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts`
  - `src/plugins/plugin-metadata-snapshot.memo.test.ts`
  - `src/plugins/current-plugin-metadata-snapshot.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: restrictive `plugins.allow` startup config passes a one-plugin scope to installed-index manifest reconstruction; conservative fallback remains unscoped for compat discovery or unmappable providers.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it asserts the actual manifest-registry load boundary without needing external plugin credentials.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none before this PR.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Gateway startup with a restrictive plugin allowlist can avoid reading unrelated installed plugin manifests when the startup scope is safe to prove from the Installed Plugin Index. Unsafe configs keep existing unscoped behavior.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Installed Index (96 plugins) -> read 96 manifests -> startup plan -> openai

After:
Installed Index (96 plugins) -> conservative scope ["openai"] -> read 1 manifest -> startup plan -> openai
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux testserver disposable run
- Runtime/container: Node v22.22.2, pnpm 11.1.2
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): synthetic installed plugin fixture; no external channel account
- Relevant config (redacted): `plugins.allow=["openai"]`, `plugins.slots.memory="none"`

### Steps

1. On `origin/main`, run the diagnostic fixture through `loadPluginLookUpTable`.
2. Apply this patch.
3. Run the same diagnostic fixture again.
4. Run focused regression tests, format, typecheck, and targeted lint.

### Expected

- Startup still selects `openai`.
- Manifest reconstruction only reads the scoped startup plugin after the patch.
- Conservative fallback paths remain unscoped when the Installed Plugin Index cannot prove the mapping.

### Actual

- Before: `manifestPluginCount=96`, `startupPluginIds=["openai"]`.
- After: `manifestPluginCount=1`, `loadedPluginIds=["openai"]`, `startupPluginIds=["openai"]`.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [x] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Validation:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/status.summary.redaction.test.ts src/plugins/plugin-lookup-table.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-registry-installed.test.ts src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-plugins.test.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.test.ts src/plugins/plugin-metadata-snapshot.memo.test.ts src/plugins/current-plugin-metadata-snapshot.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-contract-eligibility.test.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts
10 files / 269 tests passed

corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugins/plugin-metadata-snapshot.ts src/plugins/plugin-metadata-snapshot.types.ts src/plugins/plugin-lookup-table.ts src/plugins/manifest-registry-installed.ts src/plugins/effective-plugin-ids.ts src/plugins/gateway-startup-plugin-ids.ts src/gateway/server-startup-plugins.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.ts src/commands/status.summary.redaction.test.ts
passed

git diff --check
passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
passed

node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json <11 changed files>
0 warnings / 0 errors

pnpm check:changed

```

Additional note: `origin/main @ a13468320c` failed `test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json` before this patch because `status.summary.redaction.test.ts` had not been updated for the newer `SessionStatus` fields. This PR includes that test-only fixture update so `check:changed` can pass cleanly on the rebased branch.

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: before/after runtime manifest reconstruction count on the real OpenClaw metadata lookup path; restrictive allowlist scope; compat discovery fallback; unmappable provider fallback; scoped/unscoped memo separation; configless scoped snapshot reuse rejection.
- Edge cases checked: disabled plugin entries are removed from the scope, direct configured channels/providers only scope when the Installed Plugin Index can map them, scope ids outside the index fall back to unscoped loading.
- What I did **not** verify: real production provider/channel credentials or long-running gateway service startup.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: overly aggressive startup scoping could hide a plugin whose startup intent is only manifest-visible.
  - Mitigation: scope is only used when allowlists/config entries/slots/direct channel/provider/harness ids can be mapped from the Installed Plugin Index; ambiguous compat, provider, channel, or out-of-index cases return to unscoped manifest reconstruction.

target.title: fix(infra): tolerate chmod EPERM on K8s fsGroup-mounted PVC
target.body:
## Summary

On Kubernetes deployments with `fsGroup`-mounted PVCs, `chmod(dir, 0o700)` fails with EPERM because the state directory is owned by root (with group ownership set to the fsGroup GID). This crashes exec approvals, task registry, task-flow registry, and plugin-state initialization on startup.

This PR extracts a shared `tryChmodSync` helper that:
1. Tries `chmodSync(target, mode)` as before
2. On EPERM/EACCES/EROFS: verifies the process can actually access the path via `accessSync`
3. If accessible → tolerates the failure (the fsGroup grants adequate permissions via group bits)
4. If not accessible → rethrows the original error

## Root cause

```
EPERM: operation not permitted, chmod '/home/openclaw/.openclaw'
```

K8s `fsGroup` volumes are owned by `root:<fsGroup>` with setgid bit. The container process runs as a non-root UID in the fsGroup, so it has full access via group membership but cannot `chmodSync` a root-owned directory.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/infra/chmod.ts` | New `tryChmodSync` helper — chmod with EPERM/EACCES/EROFS tolerance |
| `src/infra/chmod.test.ts` | 9 tests covering all error codes and access verification paths |
| `src/infra/exec-approvals.ts` | Use `tryChmodSync` instead of raw `chmodSync` |
| `src/tasks/task-registry.store.sqlite.ts` | Use `tryChmodSync` |
| `src/tasks/task-flow-registry.store.sqlite.ts` | Use `tryChmodSync` |
| `src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.sqlite.ts` | Use `tryChmodSync` |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/chmod.test.ts
 ✓ infra  ../../src/infra/chmod.test.ts (12 tests) 16ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  12 passed (12)
   Duration  152ms
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `chmod(dir, 0o700)` throws EPERM on K8s fsGroup-mounted PVC, crashing exec approvals and state initialization. The helper tolerates EPERM when access is verified, and rejects only the genuinely dangerous case (unexpected other-WRITE permission).
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at `bfadbe3e37`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`. Also reproduced on OrbStack Kubernetes v1.33.9.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main (`a002c416c7`), `chmodSync(dir, 0o700)` on a K8s fsGroup volume throws EPERM and crashes the gateway on startup. Test file `src/infra/chmod.test.ts` does not exist on upstream/main.

### Permission boundary policy (updated in `bfadbe3e37`)

K8s fsGroup volumes commonly set directories to mode `2775` (setgid + rwxrwxr-x). The helper now:
- **Accepts** `2775` (other has read+execute but NOT write) ✅
- **Accepts** `2755`, `2751`, etc. (other read/execute without write) ✅
- **Rejects** `2777` or any mode with unexpected other-WRITE (`0o002`) ❌ — this signals a genuine security misconfiguration

This boundary aligns with the real K8s fsGroup behavior: volume plugins set read+execute on `other`, but never write.

### Live K8s reproduction

Pod spec:
```yaml
securityContext:
  runAsUser: 1000
  runAsGroup: 1000
  fsGroup: 1000
volumes:
  - name: state
    emptyDir: {}  # simulates fsGroup PVC
```

Terminal output from `kubectl exec`:
```
=== K8s fsGroup EPERM Reproduction ===
Node: v24.15.0
User: uid=1000(node) gid=1000(node) groups=1000(node)

--- State directory (fsGroup mount) ---
drwxrwsr-x 1 root node  0 May 20 07:00 .openclaw

Owner: 0:1000
Mode: 0o2775

--- Main branch: chmod(dir, 0o700) ---
chmod FAILED: EPERM EPERM: operation not permitted, chmod '/home/openclaw/.openclaw'

--- PR fix: tryChmodSync(dir, 0o700) ---
chmod failed with EPERM - checking access...
accessSync(R_OK|W_OK|X_OK) PASSED ✅
actualOtherWrite=0, requestedOtherWrite=0 → tolerating
→ tryChmodSync tolerates EPERM, gateway starts normally

--- Verify: can create state files ---
Write+Read: ✅ {"test":true}
Cleanup: ✅
```

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/chmod.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```text
 ✓ infra  ../../src/infra/chmod.test.ts (12 tests) 16ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  12 passed (12)
   Duration  152ms
```

- **Observed result after fix**: All 12 chmod tests pass — EPERM tolerance with accessSync fallback, K8s fsGroup mode 2775 accepted, world-writable (other-WRITE) rejected, and normal chmod behavior preserved.
- **What was not tested**: Full OpenClaw gateway startup inside a live K8s cluster. The chmod behavior is validated via mocked fs calls and real K8s `kubectl exec` reproduction.

Closes #83619
